Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **3/10** WARNING (spot price action still orderly) vs VIX Spike tier **2/4** MEDIUM with **60%** prob, skew active, curve in backwardation
Spot says calm, options say pay up for protection. Trust the VIX complex here. Backwardation and active skew are forward-looking and they lead realized stress, while the Crash Index reads what already printed.
⚠ These two disagree: Regime State **40** TRANSITION with MIXED bias vs Macro Regime BROADENING at **30%** confidence
One model sees a regime in flux, the other sees participation widening. Trust the transition read. Broadening at **30%** confidence is a weak signal, and the divergence jaws score of **100** confirms the cross-asset wiring is shifting.
Today's tension
Spot tape is calm while the options market is paying up for a vol expansion.
Bull
Crash Index sits at **3/10** with the Dow leading the US at **+0.9%** over five days, and the contrarian read at **47** says you can still accumulate into weakness. Divergence composite is in the **CALM** zone at **41.9%** diverging with a net bias of **+10**, so the underlying tape is not breaking.
Bear
VIX Spike sits at tier **2/4** with a **60%** probability, skew active and the curve in backwardation, which is the options market paying up for near-term protection. NDX is the US laggard at **-1.9%** and KOSPI bled **-7.1%** in five days, so the leadership is narrowing while the hedges get bid.
The tell: Watch the VIX term structure. If backwardation resolves and skew cools, the bull case wins; if a fourth crash signal flips on with backwardation still active, the bear case takes over.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The setup today is calm price with an anxious options market, and that gap is the entire story. Crash Index prints 3/10 in WARNING, Regime State sits at 40 in TRANSITION with a MIXED bias, and Contrarian is at 47 in partial ACCUMULATE. At the same time VIX Spike is tier 2/4 at 60% probability with skew active and the curve in backwardation, and the Mother Jaws divergence composite is pinned at 100. The spot tape has not broken, but the wiring under it is being rerouted in real time.

For positioning, you lean long with a hedge on, you do not press. The contrarian 47 and a CALM divergence zone at 41.9% diverging with net bias +10 say there is still demand for risk under the surface, and the Dow leading the US at +0.9% over five days backs that up. The NDX laggard at -1.9% tells you leadership is rotating away from the long-duration growth complex, so trim mega-cap tech beta and rebuild exposure in the cyclicals and broad-market sleeves the divergence pairs (xly/xlp flipping to +0.66, spy/rsp flipping to +0.87) are pointing at. Pay for the tail through the VIX complex while it is still cheap relative to where backwardation usually goes.

The main risk is that backwardation and skew are early, not wrong. A fourth crash signal activating, or the T1 x HYG confluence flipping live, would mean credit is catching the options market's anxiety and you cut gross. The single reading that flips the stance is the VIX term structure: backwardation resolving back to contango with skew cooling is the all-clear to add; backwardation deepening with NDX leading lower is the cue to get small.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence, which is a thesis the market is auditioning, not a fact you trade like a religion. Broadening means leadership is widening out from the mega-cap growth complex into the equal-weight and cyclical sleeves, and the divergence book backs it: spy/rsp correlation jumped from -0.15 to +0.87, xly/xlp went from -0.06 to +0.66, and the Dow leads the US at +0.9% over five days while NDX lags at -1.9%.

Position for it by tilting from cap-weighted to equal-weight, adding a cyclical and small-cap leg, and trimming the long-duration tech beta that has carried the year. Keep the VIX hedge on because regime confidence at 30% with VIX backwardation active is a thin edge, and a single tape down-day in mega-cap tech can pull the whole composite with it.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

Cross-asset wiring is being rerouted even though the headline composite reads CALM. A divergence pair tracks two assets that usually move together (say LQD and IEF, two high-grade bond proxies) and flags when their rolling correlation drifts from its normal regime.

The composite is at 41.9% of pairs diverging with a net bias of +10 (14 bearish vs 4 bullish drifts across 43 pairs), which is the CALM zone in level but the Mother Jaws score is pinned at 100. Under the hood the moves are violent: lqd/ief snapped from -0.10 to +0.87, spy/rsp flipped from -0.15 to +0.87, hg/cu vs gold went from +0.04 to +0.84, and the 2s10s pair inverted in correlation from +0.67 to -0.56.

Plain English: defensive bonds and credit are now moving together again, broad-market and equal-weight are syncing back up (which is the broadening signal), copper and gold are both bid (reflation plus haven), and the curve is decoupling from its old driver. This is the chassis under a transition, and it argues for owning the broad-market sleeve over the cap-weighted one and keeping a real-asset leg on.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

No tier-one confluences are live, which is the most useful number on the page. T1 x HYG (VIX backwardation with credit stress), T1 x B2 (backwardation with small-cap drawdown), and T1 x JPY (backwardation with yen safe-haven flow) are all inactive.

Backwardation is doing the heavy lifting in the options market by itself, but credit, small caps, and the yen are not confirming. That tells you the vol bid is mechanical and hedge-driven rather than a coordinated risk-off across asset classes.

You stay long the broad market with a VIX hedge on, and you do not size down the book yet. The trigger to cut gross is the first of these three flipping live; HYG widening alongside backwardation is the one that ends the calm.

✅

Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.

Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

The 24/7 tape is split, and the splits matter for the US open. Asia is heavy with KOSPI down -7.1% over five days and the ASX off only -0.7%, so Korea is carrying a tech and semiconductor bruise that lines up with NDX as the US laggard at -1.9%.

Europe shows a defensive lead, with SSMI +2.9% (Swiss, heavy in staples and pharma) over a DAX print of -1.3%, which says European money is rotating to quality and away from the cyclical export beta. The US mirrors the rotation: Dow leads at +0.9%, NDX lags at -1.9%, the same broadening trade the divergence book is flagging.

Crypto is the outlier, with SOL +10.2% while BTC sits at -1.4%, so the speculative bid is alive in alt-coins but not in the bellwether. The read for today is to fade strength in mega-cap tech into the open, lean into the cyclical and equal-weight rotation, and treat the SOL move as a single-name story, not a risk-on green light for the whole book.

Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 3/10 · WARNING — 3+ signals, active surveillance
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Calm price with a bid for protection is a coherent state, not a contradiction to wave away. Crash Index at 3/10 reads what already printed in spot, breadth, and credit; VIX Spike tier 2/4 at 60% with skew active and the curve in backwardation reads what the options market is willing to pay for the next two to four weeks.

Backwardation means near-dated VIX futures trade above longer-dated ones, which is the options market saying it expects more realized volatility soon than later, and active skew means out-of-the-money puts are bid relative to calls. The honest read is that dealers and hedgers are positioning for a vol expansion the cash tape has not delivered yet, and that gap usually closes one of two ways: the options bid fades and the calm wins, or spot catches down to where vol is priced.

Trust the VIX complex here because it leads; the Crash Index will follow if the third leg, adaptive, also flips on.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 3/10 — a few early signals are stirring; watch whether more line up.

Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (1)
• Dollar wrecking ball
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
